<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [warning] Two web viewer unit tests (graph-interaction, graph-zoom) are algorithmically grouped with root-level dev scripts due to having no cross-file imports. They should be associated with the Web Dashboard zone to avoid misclassification.
[observation] [info] scripts/hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs is a useful standalone audit tool that reads sourcevision output — its presence here is correct but could benefit from a README entry or package.json script alias so it is discoverable.
[observation] [warning] scripts/test-zone-consistency.mjs is effectively empty (1 line). If it was a planned utility, it should be completed or deleted to keep the scripts directory meaningful.
[pattern] [warning] The two misclassified web viewer tests (graph-interaction, graph-zoom) cause web-dashboard zone test coverage to be undercounted in any metric derived from zone membership, creating a silent reporting inaccuracy.
[pattern] [warning] developer-utilities is structurally defined by absence of imports rather than shared purpose. It will accumulate unrelated files on every analysis pass unless zone pins are used to anchor the legitimate member files (hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs) and expel the misclassified ones.
[anti-pattern] [warning] Zone spans three unrelated directory namespaces (scripts/, tests/, packages/web/tests/). A zone whose membership is defined purely by graph isolation rather than shared purpose will silently absorb any future file with no cross-imports, progressively degrading all derived metrics. Apply zone pins immediately to expel misclassified test files and anchor the legitimate script members.
[anti-pattern] [warning] scripts/test-zone-consistency.mjs is an empty 1-line file providing no utility. Its presence in a zone named 'developer-utilities' implies intent that was never fulfilled; it should be implemented or deleted to prevent false confidence in zone-consistency tooling coverage.
[suggestion] [warning] Per-file call-graph density of 34.5 edges/file is the highest in the codebase and is driven entirely by the two misclassified web viewer test files. Correcting zone membership via zone pins would expose the true (near-zero) call density of the legitimate script files and eliminate the misleading cohesion signal. Act on this before the next analysis pass to prevent the anomaly from influencing AI-generated zone health summaries.
[suggestion] [info] hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs has a file-system runtime dependency on .sourcevision/callgraph.json with zero static coupling signal. Add an inline comment or README annotation documenting this dependency so future developers and automated tools can account for it — otherwise the script silently fails if sourcevision has not been run first.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- scripts/hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs reads .sourcevision/callgraph.json to detect circular call patterns in the hench package — useful for architectural audits
- scripts/test-zone-consistency.mjs is an empty stub (1 line) and should either be populated or removed
- packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/graph-interaction.test.ts and graph-zoom.test.ts test the SVG GraphRenderer in isolation and logically belong with Web Dashboard, not here
- Two web viewer unit tests (graph-interaction, graph-zoom) are algorithmically grouped with root-level dev scripts due to having no cross-file imports. They should be associated with the Web Dashboard zone to avoid misclassification.
- scripts/test-zone-consistency.mjs is effectively empty (1 line). If it was a planned utility, it should be completed or deleted to keep the scripts directory meaningful.
- scripts/hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs is a useful standalone audit tool that reads sourcevision output — its presence here is correct but could benefit from a README entry or package.json script alias so it is discoverable.
- The zone functions as an entropy sink: any future isolated script or misplaced test file with no shared imports will be absorbed here by community detection, progressively diluting its semantic meaning
- Misclassifying graph-interaction.test.ts and graph-zoom.test.ts here creates a test-coverage blind spot in web-dashboard zone metrics — zone-level test-to-source ratios for web-dashboard are understated by exactly 2 files
- developer-utilities is structurally defined by absence of imports rather than shared purpose. It will accumulate unrelated files on every analysis pass unless zone pins are used to anchor the legitimate member files (hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs) and expel the misclassified ones.
- The two misclassified web viewer tests (graph-interaction, graph-zoom) cause web-dashboard zone test coverage to be undercounted in any metric derived from zone membership, creating a silent reporting inaccuracy.
- The zone spans three distinct directory hierarchies (scripts/, tests/ root, packages/web/tests/) with no semantic relationship — this is the widest namespace spread of any zone in the codebase and confirms it is a pure graph-topology artifact, not an intentional boundary.
- scripts/test-zone-consistency.mjs being an empty stub inside the zone that is itself a zone-consistency problem is a self-referential failure: the tool intended to catch zone drift is inert while living inside a known misclassification.
- The 138 internal call-graph edges attributed to developer-utilities are inflated by the two misclassified web viewer test files — the call graph metrics for the legitimate members (two script files) are much lower, making the cohesion score of 1 structurally misleading for the actual scripts sub-population.
- Zone spans three unrelated directory namespaces (scripts/, tests/, packages/web/tests/). A zone whose membership is defined purely by graph isolation rather than shared purpose will silently absorb any future file with no cross-imports, progressively degrading all derived metrics. Apply zone pins immediately to expel misclassified test files and anchor the legitimate script members.
- scripts/test-zone-consistency.mjs is an empty 1-line file providing no utility. Its presence in a zone named 'developer-utilities' implies intent that was never fulfilled; it should be implemented or deleted to prevent false confidence in zone-consistency tooling coverage.
- Per-file call-graph density is 138 edges / 4 files = 34.5 avg — the highest of any zone in this batch. Script files (.mjs) typically have single-digit call counts; the anomaly is entirely explained by the two misclassified graph-rendering test files. After correct zone membership, the legitimate script population likely drops below 5 edges per file, making the published cohesion score and edge count both artifacts of misclassification rather than properties of the scripts themselves.
- hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs couples to .sourcevision/callgraph.json via a file-system read, not an import. This is a silent runtime dependency on sourcevision output that produces zero coupling signal in static analysis — a third category of invisible coupling (alongside subprocess spawning and shared mutable state) present in the codebase with no annotation or enforcement mechanism.

- hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs has a file-system runtime dependency on .sourcevision/callgraph.json with zero static coupling signal.
